title: Lavender milk bath soak
subtitle: A calming bath soak with whole milk powder, Epsom salts, and dried lavender
excerpt: Whole milk powder softens bath water and leaves skin feeling smooth. Epsom salts deliver magnesium and ease sore muscles. Dried lavender flowers float on the surface and fragrance the steam. This blend dissolves easily and leaves no residue in the tub.
sourceNotes: Milk bath formula from Nerys Purchon, Handbook of Natural Beauty (2001). Epsom salts skin use from the British Dermatological Society review. Lavender EO usage from Essential Oil Safety, Tisserand and Young (2014).

recipe.batchNotes: Scale proportionally. Store in a large jar and scoop out approximately 80 g per bath. Or portion into smaller jars for gifting.
recipe.makeAheadNotes: Make a large batch ahead of time. The lavender flowers are best kept in the main jar and allowed to float freely when poured into the bath.
recipe.shelfLifeNotes: 12 months in a sealed container away from moisture. The milk powder and lavender flowers are the moisture-sensitive components. Do not add a wet scoop to the jar: keep a dry spoon or scoop dedicated to the container.

glossaryTerms.definition: Magnesium sulphate crystals. In a bath, they dissolve to release magnesium, which is absorbed through the skin. Used in bath soaks for muscle relaxation and as a mild exfoliant. Food-grade or pharmaceutical-grade only. Not interchangeable with table salt.

content.text: Whole milk powder contains fat, protein, and lactose. The fat component gives bath water a softer feel on the skin; the proteins are mildly emollient. The formula dissolves completely in a hot bath with no residue to clean from the tub.
make up the bulk of the blend and add the physical sensation of a mineral bath. The dried lavender flowers float decoratively and release their residual fragrance in the steam.

Use approximately 80 g per bath. Pour into running water, not still water, to help the milk powder dissolve. The bath water will turn slightly opaque and milky: this is the intended effect. Suitable for sensitive skin, including children over two years. Not suitable for those with a milk allergy.

items.prepNote: Full-fat spray-dried milk; softens the bath water
Dried Lavandula angustifolia flowers; stir through last
Enhances the lavender fragrance; add drop by drop to the salts before blending

content.text: Add the lavender essential oil to the Epsom salts one drop at a time, rubbing through the crystals with a spoon between additions to distribute evenly and prevent clumping. Stir in the whole milk powder. Finally, fold in the dried lavender flowers. Transfer to a sealed jar. To use, scoop approximately 80 g into a running hot bath.

12 months in a sealed jar away from steam and moisture. Use a dedicated dry scoop: a damp hand in the jar will cause the milk powder to clump and develop an off smell. The lavender fragrance fades after 6 months; add a few drops of lavender essential oil to refresh.
